title: Trigonometry
description: Explore sine, cosine, tangent, the unit circle, and conversion functions to resolve rotation vectors.
learningObjectives: Calculate angles using inverse trigonometric functions (atan2)
Resolve angles into directional 2D vector coordinates using sin/cos
Explain unit circle boundaries for rotation checks

sections.title: Sine, Cosine, and Direction Vectors
sections.content:
Trigonometry allows you to translate between angles and directions. In 2D game structures, a character facing an angle θ has a forward direction vector of `(cos(θ), sin(θ))`. 

To calculate the angle from a player to a mouse target, the function `atan2(y, x)` is used instead of standard `atan` because it automatically handles the signs of both x and y coordinates to determine the correct quadrant from -π to +π.

sections.title: Calculating Target Angle using atan2
